issue.title: Kill dual status encoding for releases: location is sole truth, doctor auto-fix

successCriteria.description: No desktop lifecycle/display decision is made from the release JSON status field; isReleaseCompleted relies solely on the location-derived archived flag, pinned by updated tests
tiki_doctor exposes residual stale-active archived defs as an actionable finding with a normalize_archived_releases command that rewrites them to status:shipped; cargo test pins the normalizer
The Diagnostics panel offers a Fix action that runs the normalizer and reloads; the finding is reframed from permanent-info to actionable-when-residue-exists; vitest pins the summary logic
check-release-readiness.mjs warns when an archived release def's status != shipped (the v0.9.0 footgun catch), with a framework test

Existing release tests stay green (location-derivation, #259 archived-survives-serialization, archivedButActive compute, includeArchived guard); full pnpm test + build green; dogfood regen if a canonical script changed

phases.title: Desktop UI: location-only release completion
phases.content:
Read `.tiki/research/release-status-single-source.md` first.

Make the location-derived `archived` flag the SOLE signal for release completion in the desktop:
1. `apps/desktop/src/utils/releaseDisplayStatus.ts` `isReleaseCompleted()`: change from `archived || status===shipped || status===completed` to `Boolean(release.archived)` only. Update the function's doc comment to state location is the sole truth (status no longer consulted; #276). Since #259 guarantees `archived` always survives IPC (Rust stamps it from location on every load), the status disjuncts were dead/footgun fallback.
2. AUDIT the rest of `apps/desktop/src` for any OTHER place that branches on a release's `status` field for a display/gating decision (grep `\.status` near release usage: ReleasesSection, TikiReleaseDetail, ReleaseDialog gating, etc.). ReleaseDialog create/edit WRITING status:'active' is correct (a live release) — leave writes. Only redirect READ-side DECISIONS to `archived`. Report what you found; redirect any genuine status-based decision, leave informational displays.
3. `apps/desktop/src/utils/__tests__/releaseDisplayStatus.test.ts`: update cases. The old 'falls back to status=shipped/completed when archived absent' cases now assert: archived flag is the only thing that marks completed; a def with status:'shipped' but archived:false/undefined is NOT completed (location is truth — and in practice the Rust loader always sets archived, so this models the invariant). Keep the 'archived=true regardless of status' case. Add a case: archived:true + status:'active' (the residue) → still completed (location wins).
Gates: `pnpm -C apps/desktop test` green; `pnpm build` (tsc -b) clean.
verification: isReleaseCompleted uses only archived (location); doc updated
No other desktop status-based lifecycle decision remains (audit reported)
releaseDisplayStatus.test.ts updated: archived is sole completion signal; residue case (archived+status:active) → completed
phases.summary: isReleaseCompleted() → Boolean(release.archived) only (dropped status==='shipped'/'completed' disjuncts); doc rewritten (location is sole truth, #259 guarantees archived survives IPC). Audit: isReleaseCompleted was the ONLY completion-decision site; its callers (TikiReleaseDetail:66, ReleasesSection:304) inherit the fix. Other release.status==='active' usages (DependencyGraph, KanbanFilters, IssueFormModal, ReleasesSection not_planned) are "which release is live" filters NOT completion decisions — correct, left as-is. releaseDisplayStatus.test.ts updated: archived sole signal (status:shipped+archived:false → NOT completed), archived:true+status:active residue → completed. Gates: desktop vitest 212, tsc -b clean. testResults: { framework: "vitest", command: "pnpm -C apps/desktop test", passed: 212, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }.

phases.title: Doctor: normalize_archived_releases command + actionable finding + Fix button
phases.content:
Read the research doc. Add a one-shot normalizer + make the doctor finding actionable.
1. Rust: new `#[tauri::command] pub fn normalize_archived_releases(tiki_path: Option<String>) -> Result<usize, String>` in commands.rs — scans `releases/archive/*.json`, for each whose parsed status != Shipped, rewrite the file with status set to Shipped (preserve all other fields; atomic write via the existing atomic_write/fs_utils helper). Returns count fixed. Read-failures on a single file skip it (never Err the whole op for one bad file). Register in the invoke_handler (generate_handler!). Keep `archivedButActive` computation as-is in tiki_doctor (it stays the signal source).
2. cargo test (`cargo test --lib` from src-tauri): a fixture with two archived defs (one status:active, one status:shipped) + one active-location def → normalize rewrites ONLY the stale archived one to shipped, returns 1, leaves the shipped archived + active-location defs untouched. Assert the rewritten file parses with status shipped.
3. Frontend `diagnosticsSummary.ts`: the `archivedButActive` count currently surfaces as a neutral info finding. Reframe: when count>0, surface it as an ACTIONABLE finding (a new finding level or a flag the panel can act on) labeled e.g. 'N archived release(s) have a stale "active" status — Normalize to fix'. It must STILL NOT flip top-level status to "warnings" (it's cosmetic residue, not drift) — keep status healthy; the actionability is a Fix affordance, not an alarm. Add an `actionable`/`fixable` marker to that finding's shape so the panel can render a button. Update `diagnosticsSummary.test.ts` accordingly (count>0 → actionable finding present, status stays healthy; count==0 → no finding).
4. `DiagnosticsPanel.tsx`: when the report has archivedButActive>0, render a 'Normalize archived releases' button that `invoke('normalize_archived_releases', { tikiPath })` then re-runs `load()`. Minimal, mirrors the existing Refresh button pattern. (Visual; note for user eyes but reuses existing button styling.)
Gates: `cargo test --lib` green incl. the new test + the #259/location tests; `pnpm -C apps/desktop test` green; `pnpm build` clean. `cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings` clean for your additions.
verification: normalize_archived_releases rewrites only stale-active archived defs to shipped (cargo test, returns count)
registered in invoke_handler; #259 + location tests still green
diagnosticsSummary reframes archivedButActive>0 as actionable but never flips status to warnings (vitest)
DiagnosticsPanel shows a Normalize button wired to the command
cargo --lib + desktop vitest + build green; clippy clean
phases.summary: Rust normalize_archived_releases(tiki_path)->Result<usize,String> in commands.rs: scans releases/archive/*.json, edits ONLY the status key via serde_json::Value (preserves all other fields), sets stale != shipped → "shipped", skips already-shipped + per-file failures, atomic_write, returns count; registered in lib.rs invoke_handler. cargo test normalize_archived_releases_fixes_only_stale_active_archived_defs (stale→shipped keeps version/issues, shipped+active-location untouched), cargo --lib 60→61, clippy clean, #259+location guards green. diagnosticsSummary.ts: Finding gained optional action?:"normalizeArchivedReleases"; archivedButActive>0 stays level:info (never flips status to warnings) but carries the action + "Normalize to fix" message; count==0 absent; vitest 9→11. DiagnosticsPanel.tsx: "Normalize archived releases" button (canNormalize when a finding has the action) → invoke normalize_archived_releases then load(); +.diagnostics-actions css. Build clean. VISUAL (panel button) needs user eyes in tauri:dev. testResults: { framework: "cargo+vitest", command: "cargo test --lib + pnpm -C apps/desktop test", passed: 273, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }.

phases.title: Gate: check-release-readiness warns on archived def status != shipped
phases.content:
Read the research doc. Make the pre-release gate catch the v0.9.0 footgun.
1. `packages/framework/scripts/check-release-readiness.mjs`: when it resolves a release def from the `archive/` location, additionally check the def's `status` field; if it is present and != 'shipped', emit a WARNING (soft — add to a warnings list, do NOT necessarily fail exit unless the script already treats this class as hard; match the script's existing soft-vs-hard convention — prefer soft/warn so it informs without blocking, since location is already truth). The message: name the version + 'archived def status is "<x>", expected "shipped" — run Normalize (Diagnostics) or it will mislead status-field readers'. Keep it read-only (no rewrite in the gate).
2. `packages/framework/__tests__/check-release-readiness.test.mjs`: add a case — an archived def with status:'active' → the readiness output includes the warning (and the overall pass/fail behaves per the soft convention). A shipped archived def → no warning.
3. If check-release-readiness.mjs is in the dogfood canonical scripts set (plugin-distribution parity), the final gate (run by the parent after this phase) will regen; note whether you changed a dogfood-copied script so the parent regenerates `.claude/tiki/scripts/`.
Gates: `pnpm -C packages/framework test` green for check-release-readiness.test.mjs (note: dogfood-parity may go red if check-release-readiness.mjs is a copied script — the parent runs install.js at the final gate; attribute it).
verification: check-release-readiness warns when an archived def's status != shipped; shipped → no warning
test case added (active archived def → warning; shipped → none)
soft/warn convention matched (informs, doesn't spuriously hard-fail)
noted whether a dogfood-copied script changed (for the parent's regen)
phases.summary: check-release-readiness.mjs (repo-root scripts/, NOT dogfood-copied → no regen impact) gained an archive-location check: when a def resolves from .../archive/... and its status != "shipped", push a soft WARNING (existing warnings[] channel — printed, never affects ok/exit, matching the script's soft convention): names version + status + "run Normalize (Diagnostics)". Read-only (gate flags, doctor fixes). check-release-readiness.test.mjs: archived+status:active → warning present + gate still passes; archived+status:shipped → no warning. Framework 161/161 incl. dogfood-parity green (script is outside the distributed surface). testResults: { framework: "node", command: "pnpm -C packages/framework test", passed: 161, failed: 0, skipped: 0, status: "passed" }.
